video:video.video:title: Xi's trip to North Korea will be the latest step by China to reinforce its close ties with its neighbour
video:video.video:description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week, both countries have announced, in what will be his first visit in nearly seven years.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Tirana over a coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner
video:video.video:description: Protest in Tirana over a coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner

video:video.video:title: Funeral of baby killed by Israeli forces in West Bank
video:video.video:description: A funeral was held on Saturday after Israeli troops killed a seven-month-old Palestinian baby boy when they fired at his parents’ vehicle in the occupied West Bank, the Palestinian health ministry said.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV confirms he will root for the U.S. in the upcoming soccer World Cup
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV confirmed he would root for the U.S. in the upcoming football World Cup, speaking to journalists on board the plane that took the pontiff to Spain for his weeklong trip.

video:video.video:title: Raul Castro appears for first time since he was indicted by the U.S. Justice Department
video:video.video:description: Former Cuban President Raul Castro appeared for the first time in public since he was indicted by the U.S. Justice Department.

video:video.video:title: Former first lady of France, Bernadette Chirac, dies aged 93
video:video.video:description: President Emmanuel Macron confirmed her death Saturday, saying he and his wife Brigitte had learned with “great sadness” of the passing of a woman who marked French history beside Jacques Chirac, who died in 2019, and changed the lives of millions of patients through her charitable work.
video:video.video:tag: Bernadette Thérèse Marie Chodron de Courcel

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV arrives in Madrid on tour of Spain
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has arrived in Spain for his tour of Madrid, Barcelona and the Canary Islands.

video:video.video:title: Red pandas arrive in Taipei from China in first cross-Strait zoo exchange in over a decade
video:video.video:description: A pair of Red Pandas arrived at Taiwan’s Taipei City Zoo on Saturday from China’s Shanghai Zoological Park, marking the first cross-Strait zoological exchanges between Taiwan and China in over a decade.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon considers its largest amnesty in 35 years but some are protesting
video:video.video:description: Lebanon's largest amnesty since the end of its devastating 1975-90 civil war is expected to occur in the coming weeks, once parliament approves it and the president signs it.

video:video.video:title: Iranian drone attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain after US attacks on Iran
video:video.video:description: Bahrain's government said Saturday that Iran fired ballistic missiles and drones towards it and Kuwait.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo receives royal welcome as he kicks off his six-day visit to Spain
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV received a royal welcome from King Felipe and Queen Leticia as he kicked off a six-day visit to Spain. (AP video shot by Silvia Stellacci)

video:video.video:title: Pope says he’s competing with another VIP in Madrid this weekend: Puerto Rican sensation Bad Bunny
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV acknowledged on Saturday that he was competing with another VIP in Madrid this weekend.

video:video.video:title: Shark fatally mauls spearfishing diver off Australia's Rottnest Island, police say
video:video.video:description: A shark fatally mauled a diver off an Australian tourist island on Saturday, police said.

video:video.video:title: US Defence Secretary Hegseth at D-Day ceremony in France
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth laid tributes at a memorial in France on Saturday as he attended a ceremony marking 82 years since the D-Day landings.

video:video.video:title: Colombia's presidential runoff could reshape the future of the Amazon and fossil fuel development
video:video.video:description: The upcoming Colombian presidential election between Sen. Iván Cepeda, an ally of President Gustavo Petro, and lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella, whom U.S.

video:video.video:title: Landmarks and businesses are preparing for the World Cup's economic impact in Atlanta
video:video.video:description: Atlanta is not only welcoming soccer fans during the World Cup, but also business opportunities. Georgia is building the biggest fan zone the city has seen since the Olympics, landmarks are investing in soccer-themed souvenirs, and small businesses are finding new ways to expand.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits a welfare center in Madrid run by the Caritas charity
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited a welfare center run by the Caritas charity in a deprived area of Madrid on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: A new exchange of fire with Iran in the Gulf tests the fragile ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The latest exchange of fire came as the Trump administration pressed Iran to make a deal to end the war that has strained the global economy and threatened a hunger crisis in some of the world’s most vulnerable countries.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth invokes immigration and ‘invasion’ in D-Day speech in France
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a D-Day anniversary speech on Saturday to appear to link immigration by sea to the wartime liberation of Europe, warning that the freedom won by Allied troops could prove temporary if leaders failed to defend it.

video:video.video:title: Peruvians are soon to choose their president, but neither candidate has convinced the electorate
video:video.video:description: Peru has two options when they head to the polls on Sunday: Keiko Fujimori of the Fuerza Popular political party and Roberto Sánchez of the Juntos por el Perú political party, who, out of more than 30 presidential candidates in the first round, were the top two choices.

video:video.video:title: About 500,000 people pour into a Madrid plaza for an evening prayer vigil with Pope Leo XIV
video:video.video:description: About 500,000 people, many of them young Spaniards, poured into a Madrid plaza for an evening prayer vigil with Pope Leo XIV on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim tours missile factory as his sister says no to denuclearisation
video:video.video:description: North Korean state media has shown leader Kim Jong Un visiting a missile production facility and calling for more than doubling the country's missile production capacity.

video:video.video:title: Voters in Kosovo will head to the polls this weekend for the third time in 18 months
video:video.video:description: Voters in Kosovo are heading to the polls this weekend for the third time in 18 months. Frustration is growing over a political impasse in the small Balkan country, which aspires to move closer to the European Union and NATO.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures severe winds collapsing a large tent in West Virginia University ball park
video:video.video:description: Powerful winds tossed a large white tent at a college baseball game in West Virginia in a burst of severe weather that injured five people, according to officials with West Virginia University.

video:video.video:title: Police respond to scene where multiple people were shot near a festival in Toledo, Ohio
video:video.video:description: Multiple people were shot Saturday afternoon near a community street festival in Toledo, Ohio, police said. Officers are actively searching for suspects.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Ye holds concert in the Dutch city of Arnhem despite opposition from Jewish groups
video:video.video:description: A concert by rapper Ye, formerly Kanye West, took place in the eastern Dutch city of Arnhem on Saturday despite vocal opposition from Jewish groups and many Dutch lawmakers.

video:video.video:title: Armenian PM and opposition vote in election, amid Russian pressure to prevent drift toward West
video:video.video:description: Armenians are voting Sunday in parliamentary elections as the incumbent government, under mounting Russian pressure, seeks to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West.

video:video.video:title: The Nigerian army frees 360 abducted people in northeastern Borno state
video:video.video:description: The Nigerian army said Sunday it freed 360 people abducted by Boko Haram in southern Borno, in the northeastern part of the country.

video:video.video:title: Soccer great Pelé's legacy shines on in his one-time home ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup approaches, the image of one of Brazil's most recognizable soccer players lives on in Santos, the one-time home of Edson Arantes do Nascimento, far better known as Pelé.

video:video.video:title: Pope honours Spain's tradition of religious devotion as a 'school of faith' during Madrid mass
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV honoured Spain's centuries-old tradition of religious devotion on Sunday during a Mass before a million people.

video:video.video:title: In Brazil’s Cerrado region, Indigenous fire practices reshape wildfire strategy
video:video.video:description: The Xerente people in northern Tocantins state have mastered controlled burning to manage the land. Their expertise is being incorporated into Brazil's public policy as authorities combine scientific research with traditional knowledge.

video:video.video:title: Iranian football team arrives in Mexico for World Cup preparations
video:video.video:description: The Iranian football team arrived in Mexico amid high security early on Sunday for its World Cup preparations.

video:video.video:title: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs days after US-supported ceasefire deal
video:video.video:description: Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs without warning on Sunday, days after a ceasefire agreement in Washington went into effect and despite a US request not to attack Lebanon's capital.

video:video.video:title: At least one Israeli killed in shooting attack in central Israel, police and paramedics say
video:video.video:description: A Palestinian man with Israeli citizenship has opened fire in central Israel, killing a reservist and wounding five other people.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Lima as Peruvians choose between two presidential hopefuls with very different views
video:video.video:description: Peruvians cast their ballots on Sunday in an election that will allow voters to choose their ninth president in just 10 years, either the conservative daughter of a disgraced former president or a nationalist congressman.

video:video.video:title: Armenians vote in general election under Russian pressure aimed at preventing a drift toward West
video:video.video:description: Armenians voted in a parliamentary election on Sunday as the incumbent government, under mounting Russian pressure, sought to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West.

video:video.video:title: With rising crime on their minds, Peruvians vote for president yet again
video:video.video:description: Peruvians took to the polls on Sunday to choose between two presidential hopefuls with starkly different views. (AP video shot by: Cesar Barreto)

video:video.video:title: Prime minister and Serbian opposition leader vote in Kosovo elections
video:video.video:description: Kosovo's Prime Minister cast his ballot on Sunday as voters headed to the polls for an early parliamentary election, the third in less than 18 months, in an attempt to unlock a political impasse in the small Balkan nation.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV honors Spain's tradition at Mass attended by a million in Madrid
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV honored Spain's centuries-old tradition of religious devotion on Sunday as a “school of faith”, as he presided over a Mass before a million people and highlighted one of the most iconic expressions of Spanish popular piety with a procession over flower-petal carpets (AP video: Trisha Th

video:video.video:title: Business in the front, party in the back - Danish championships celebrates much-maligned mullet
video:video.video:description: A packed Danish crowd Saturday celebrated the much-maligned but enduring mullet hairstyle at the country’s annual Mullet Championships. (AP Video by James Brooks)

video:video.video:title: Business in the front, party in the back - Danish championships celebrates much-maligned mullet
video:video.video:description: Business in the front, party in the back - a packed Danish crowd Saturday celebrated the much-maligned but enduring mullet hairstyle at the country’s annual Mullet Championships.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians suffer from lack of proper toilets across Gaza's vast tent cities
video:video.video:description: Palestinians have been suffering from lack of proper toilets across Gaza's vast tent cities. In their bare-bones tent in southern Gaza, Mostafa Shaaban built his family’s makeshift toilet behind a curtain in a corner.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, June 7: Israel strikes Beirut days after ceasefire takes effect; Police search for suspects after Ohio festival shooting; Over 1 million see Pope Leo XIV in Madrid, Spain; Trump to attend NBA Finals Game 3 in New York.

video:video.video:title: Starmer hosts Zelenskyy, Macron and Merz for talks in London
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy insisted Sunday that Europe must play a key role in any negotiations to end the war with Russia as he arrived for talks in London with staunch European allies in the wake of a Russian drone strike that killed three people.

video:video.video:title: Sánchez, Fujimori vote in runoff election as EU observers deploy across Peru
video:video.video:description: Peruvian presidential candidates Roberto Sánchez and Keiko Fujimori voted in Lima on Sunday as the nation went to the polls to decide their ninth president in just 10 years.

video:video.video:title: Kelli O'Hara enjoys looseness of 'Fallen Angels'
video:video.video:description: Kelli O'Hara and Rose Byrne co-starred in the revival of "Fallen Angels" on Broadway. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Zhailon Levingston on Andrew Lloyd Webber's blessing for his 'Cats' reimagining
video:video.video:description: "Cats: The Jellicle Ball" has been nominated for nine Tony Awards. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Broadway's best dressed arrive at the 2026 Tony Awards
video:video.video:description: Stars walked the blue carpet at Radio City Music Hall for the 79th annual Tony Awards. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO throws ceremonial first pitch at S.Korean baseball game
video:video.video:description: Nvidia founder and CEO Jensen Huang threw the ceremonial first pitch at a South Korean baseball game in Seoul.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates art, culture and sport at Madrid cultural event
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV watched a twirling Flamenco dancer and received a badminton racket from two athletes at an event in Madrid honoring art, culture and sport.

video:video.video:title: Daniel Radcliffe has no plans to stop doing theater
video:video.video:description: Daniel Radcliffe has been in six Broadway productions. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Julia Louis-Dreyfus on June Squibb's miraculous career
video:video.video:description: Julia Louis-Dreyfus says she's inspired by fellow actor June Squibb. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Broadway stars: theater wins in a battle with AI
video:video.video:description: Broadway stars say AI can do a lot, but it can't replace the experience of live theater. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Iranian missiles aimed at Israel seen over Middle Eastern sky
video:video.video:description: Missiles were seen over the middle eastern sky on Sunday after Israel said Iran has launched missiles at it in the first such bombardment since a fragile ceasefire took effect in early April, complicating mediation efforts for a deal to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Israel strikes Iran after taking missile fire, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Israel struck Iran on Monday after being targeted by missiles, while a U.S. military base in Saudi Arabia came under fire in the most serious exchange of hostilities since an April ceasefire, raising the possibility of a return to heavy fighting and complicating mediation efforts to end the war.

video:video.video:title: 7.8 magnitude earthquake shakes part of southern Philippines
video:video.video:description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake has shaken part of the southern Philippines, and a tsunami was possible on some regional coasts.

video:video.video:title: Earthquake in Philippines disrupts morning school flag ceremony
video:video.video:description: In a video shared by Mahayahay Elementary School on social media, school children were seen screaming and crying when an offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines early Monday.

video:video.video:title: Purported missiles were seen over the West Bank after a renewed exchange of fire between Israel and Iran.
video:video.video:description: Smoke from interceptions was visible and explosions were heard in Jerusalem, and what appeared to be missiles were seen over the West Bank Monday morning after a renewed exchange of fire between Israel and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Cuba's iconic antique cars sit idle as US oil blockade deepens fuel crisis
video:video.video:description: A worsening fuel crisis across Cuba is testing the island's famed "almendrones," the vintage American cars that serve as vital shared taxis and embody the island’s ingenuity and endurance.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian regions near the frontlines are seeing a rise in premature births
video:video.video:description: A rising number of babies are being born prematurely — before 37 weeks of pregnancy — in Ukraine, particularly in regions near the frontlines, where some areas have seen rates nearly double since the conflict started with Russia's invasion in 2022.

video:video.video:title: Demonstrators protest FIFA policy prohibiting pre-Islamic Revolution Iranian flags into World Cup matches
video:video.video:description: Waving the flags of pre-Islamic Revolution Iran, hundreds of protesters opposed to the current government demonstrated outside SOFI Stadium in Inglewood, California where the team's World Cup matches will be initially played.

video:video.video:title: 7.8 magnitude quake kills 12 and damages buildings in southern Philippines
video:video.video:description: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines Monday, leaving at least 12 people dead, injuring more than 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sparking a 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami along nearby coasts, officials said.

video:video.video:title: A new generation of Elvis tribute artists compete in the king's hometown
video:video.video:description: Donning dazzling jumpsuits and black hair spray, a group of young Elvis tribute artists competed against one another last week as part of the Tupelo Elvis Festival.

video:video.video:title: “Schmigadoon!" wins best musical at Tony Awards
video:video.video:description: “Schmigadoon!,” an adaptation of an Apple TV series that gently mocks big, brassy Broadway shows, won the best new musical and actor John Lithgow, 80, became the oldest man ever to win a competitive acting Tony.

video:video.video:title: 6 people hurt in stabbings at New York's Penn Station with a suspect in custody, authorities says
video:video.video:description: Authorities say six people were injured in a stabbing inside New York’s Penn Station Sunday evening. A suspect was taken into custody.

video:video.video:title: Building in General Santos City collapses amid a magnitude 7.8 earthquake in the Philippines
video:video.video:description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early Monday, causing damage in a key coastal city, knocking down power and sparking a tsunami warning on some regional coasts.
